regimeSub: Inflation at a 3-yr high (4.2%) forces a hawkish Fed pivot toward HIKES, even as the US-Iran deal cools oil and lifts risk.
themes: CPI back to 4.2% -> Fed drops cutting bias, dots now point to next move UP -> front-end repricing
US-Iran deal + Hormuz fears ease -> Brent back to $79 -> geopolitical risk premium bleeding out of risk assets
Sticky inflation + resilient 1.6% growth -> 'higher for longer' becomes 'higher from here' -> bear-flattening pressure
Gold pinned at $4177 despite easing oil -> debasement/fiscal-dominance hedge, not just war hedge

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63 vs CPI 4.2 = negative real front end; Fed behind the curve, hike risk live for Sept.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +29bp, barely positive; hike threat caps the front end, bear-flattening bias if inflation sticks.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight; VIX 16 and Iran deal keep credit complacent despite hawkish Fed — vulnerable to repricing.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 16.4, sub-trend calm on Iran de-escalation; cheap optionality into a Fed leaning hawkish.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $79 post US-Iran deal; Hormuz premium gone, but diesel/AI-power demand a sneaky upside tail.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4177 and firm even as oil falls — fiscal-dominance and Fed-independence fears, not war, are the bid.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1538 weak won; DXY ~101 contained, but hawkish Fed + KOSPI 8997 melt-up keep flows two-sided.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI leadership intact (Micron/Marvell), but power/energy bottleneck and rate threat are the new constraints.

headline: As of 2026-06-19, a US-Iran deal has defused the oil shock and juiced risk, but 4.2% inflation has flipped the Fed toward hikes — markets are pricing de-escalation and reflation at the same time.
